name: HDC-2B "Cryovein"
description: The Cryovein replaces the Borealis's single magnetron with a phased-array microwave system that electronically steers the beam through a cone-shaped sweep pattern, sublimating ice in a 2-metre radius cylinder around the borehole without any mechanical movement. This increases extraction volume per borehole by over 4× compared to the fixed-beam Borealis. Three boreholes can be in simultaneous operation from a single surface pad via a rotating turret mount. Drill depth reaches 12 metres, penetrating through regolith cap layers into the massive ice lenses found at 6–10 m depth in many permanently shadowed craters. At 10 tonnes the Cryovein remains lighter than even the smallest sifting unit, but the phased-array magnetron bank draws 10 MW — requiring a dedicated reactor-generator stack. Water output of 472 kg/hr makes it the mid-game standard for ice-rich site development.
